notes: Approximately 43 phytoplankton species were observed and grouped into 23 functional groups. For the zooplankton, about 27 species were observed and classified into 8 functional groups. The study revealed that the bottom-up effects of physical-chemical variables on some phytoplankton functional groups was stronger than the top-down effects of zooplankton. Phytoplankton species were classified into functional groups (FGs) according to (Padisák et al., 2009) and (Reynolds et al., 2002). The zooplankton species were classified into functional groups based on their body size/ length and mode of feeding (Benedetti et al., 2015; Benedetti et al., 2018; Ma et al., 2019b; Mwagona et al., 2018).
